title: Strong-scaling regression diagnosis
difficulty_justification: Branching plan: agent must compare a 16-node baseline run (job 906001) against a 128-node run (job 906044), pull telemetry for both job allocations, identify which collective dominates, and reason across slurm and telemetry evidence to attribute the slowdown.
query_text: Job 906044 ran 3x slower on 128 nodes than its 16-node baseline (job 906001). Same code, same input deck. Where is the time going at scale and what should I tell the user?

eval_criteria.gold_answer: MPI_Allreduce dominates at 128 nodes - it consumes 71% of wall time on the 128-node run versus 18% on the 16-node baseline, while per-node compute time is unchanged. Network counters show no congestion. The bottleneck is collective frequency, not bandwidth. Recommend the user reduce the all-reduce frequency (e.g. accumulate gradients across more local steps) or switch to non-blocking collectives; raising node count further will worsen the regression.

checkpoints.description: Agent retrieves details for both 906001 and 906044.
checkpoints.binary_pass_condition: trace contains slurm.job_details for both job_ids

checkpoints.description: Diagnosis names MPI_Allreduce (or equivalent collective) as the dominant cost at 128 nodes.
checkpoints.binary_pass_condition: final_answer references allreduce/collective dominance with a numeric fraction

checkpoints.description: Recommendation cites a specific code or submission change (collective frequency, non-blocking pattern, or node-count reduction).
checkpoints.binary_pass_condition: final_answer contains a concrete remediation step keyed to the bottleneck
